_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **ACTION REQUIRED — Download and operationalise the correct Scotland-specific supplemental certificates immediately.** The workspace must ensure all consignment documentation packs for cattle, sheep, and goats moving to Scotland include *two* certificates: the standard GB export certificate issued via DAERA systems, and the Scotland-specific supplemental certificate. These are separate documents; the supplemental certificate must reference the standard GB certificate number so the two are linked. Failure to include both certificates is a compliance breach that can result in rejection at destination. Deadline: **in force now; no grace period**.
  *Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free*

- **WINDOW CLOSING — Switch to the post-1 June 2026 breeding and production certificate versions before 1 June 2026.** DAERA has published preview versions of the Sheep & Goats Breeding & Production supplementary certificate and the Cattle Breeding & Production supplementary certificate labelled "1st JUNE 2026 ONWARDS – NOT YET IN USE, FOR INFO ONLY". The workspace must ensure that consignments for breeding and production animals dispatched on or after 1 June 2026 use only the new certificate versions. Using the March–31 May versions after this date will render documentation non-compliant. Deadline: **1 June 2026**.
  *Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free*

- **ACTION REQUIRED — Verify that animals for slaughter are routed only to a designated Scottish abattoir.** Where the purpose of movement is direct slaughter, the premises of destination must be an abattoir designated for slaughtering livestock from bluetongue control zones. The workspace must confirm destination abattoir designation status before booking transport or issuing shipping instructions. Deadline: **per consignment, in force now**.
  *Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free*

- **ACTION REQUIRED — Enforce a no-unloading-in-transit rule on all Scotland-bound livestock movements.** Once animals are loaded and the journey has commenced, animals must not be unloaded until arrival at the destination premises, except at scheduled stops (motorway service stations, official lay-bys on trunk/main roads, lorry parks or similar). The workspace must brief all haulage partners and issue written instructions for each Scotland-bound consignment. Deadline: **per consignment, in force now**.
  *Source: Live Animals Exports, DAERA. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/services/live-animals-exports*

- **MONITORING — Track the Scottish Government's June 2026–September 2026 high-risk season controls and update operational procedures accordingly.** From 1 June to 9 September 2026, Scotland is entering a tightened control period during which all movements of bluetongue-susceptible animals and germinal products from the BTV-3 Restricted Zone (England, Wales, Northern Ireland, Isle of Man) to Scotland will be under licence. New pre-movement PCR testing requirements and/or vaccination conditions apply to most animal categories. General licences are to be published by the Scottish Government before the control period commences; specific licences require individual APHA application. The workspace must monitor gov.scot for publication and update SOP templates accordingly.
  *Source: Movement controls from 1 June 2026, Scottish Government, March 2026. https://www.gov.scot/publications/bluetongue/pages/movement-restrictions-from-1-june-2026/*
